challenges.title: Basic File I/O
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that uses `ofstream` to write at least 3 lines of text to a file called "output.txt", then uses `ifstream` to read the file line by line and print each line to the console. Include `<fstream>` and use a `while` loop with `getline` to read the file. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'ofstream' in cpp, 'Use ofstream to write to a file'
assert 'ifstream' in cpp, 'Use ifstream to read from a file'
assert 'getline' in cpp, 'Use getline to read the file line by line'
assert 'fstream' in cpp, 'Include <fstream> for file operations'
assert '.open(' in cpp or 'ofstream ' in cpp, 'Open the file using .open() or the constructor'
assert '.close()' in cpp, 'Close the file when done'
hints: Create a file with: ofstream outFile("output.txt");
Read line by line with: while (getline(inFile, line)) { ... }

challenges.title: Binary File Operations
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that defines a struct called `Record` with an int `id` and a char array `name[50]`. Write at least 2 `Record` structs to a binary file using `.write()` with `reinterpret_cast`. Then use `seekg` to move to a specific record, read it with `.read()`, and print it. Open files in `ios::binary` mode. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'struct' in cpp and 'Record' in cpp, 'Define a struct called Record'
assert 'ios::binary' in cpp or 'binary' in cpp, 'Open files in binary mode'
assert '.write(' in cpp, 'Use .write() to write binary data'
assert '.read(' in cpp, 'Use .read() to read binary data'
assert 'reinterpret_cast' in cpp, 'Use reinterpret_cast for type conversion in binary I/O'
assert 'seekg' in cpp, 'Use seekg to move to a specific position in the file'
hints: Seek to a record with: file.seekg(recordNum * sizeof(Record), ios::beg);
